title: Jesus.
content:
Where is poor Jesus gone?
        He sits with Dives now,
     And not even the crumbs are flung
        To Lazarus below.

     Where is poor Jesus gone?
        Is he with Magdalen?
     He doles her one by one
        Her wages of shame!

     Where is poor Jesus gone?
        The good Samaritan,
     What does he there alone?
        He stabs the wounded man!

     Where is poor Jesus gone,
        The lamb they sacrificed?
     They've made God of his carrion
        And labelled it "Christ!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Francis William Lauderdale Adams, titled "Jesus.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
